meta_title: For Founders: project management to Solve no coding experience (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome no coding experience. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: PM to Solve No-Code Barriers (Expert Checklist)
content.seo_description: Overcome no-coding barriers with this expert-led project management checklist. Leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to build profitable SaaS products faster.
content.answer_block: Bridge the gap between vision and execution by treating your product as a modular system. Use project management frameworks to decompose features into atomic tasks, then leverage AI agents like Claude Code to write, debug, and deploy your codebase. This technical leverage eliminates coding barriers, accelerating your speed to market.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is filled with founders who had the vision but lacked the mechanical leverage to build it. You’re staring at a blank screen, convinced that a lack of coding experience is the wall between you and revenue. It’s not a lack of code; it’s a lack of structured architecture.

Realize that software is just logic expressed in syntax. By mastering project management as an AI-augmented founder, you transform from a bystander into a technical architect. We are going to strip away the complexity, using Antigravity and Claude Code to turn your roadmap into a functioning, revenue-ready engine.

steps.title: Define the Atomic MVP Scope
steps.description: Stop building features. Build outcomes. Decompose your SaaS into the smallest possible unit that delivers value and captures payment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Decompose a SaaS idea into a 5-step MVP roadmap focused on user authentication, database schema, core value delivery, payment integration, and deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --init --prompt 'Create a directory structure for a Next.js SaaS MVP focusing on authentication and payments.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Schema
steps.description: Your database is the foundation. If the schema is wrong, the product fails. Define your entities before writing a single line of application logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for a SaaS platform. Include tables for users, subscriptions, and core product entities. Use Prisma format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Create prisma/schema.prisma with the defined entities and run a migration preview.'

steps.title: Implement Modular Logic
steps.description: Use AI to build in isolated modules. This ensures if one component breaks, the entire engine doesn't collapse.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a modular server-side function for user authentication using NextAuth. Ensure it follows security best practices for production SaaS.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --edit 'Implement the auth module in src/lib/auth.ts and verify against existing dependencies.'

steps.title: Automate the Feedback Loop
steps.description: Integrate automated testing to catch regressions before they reach your users. A founder's time is too valuable for manual QA.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of Playwright tests for the core checkout flow. Focus on edge cases like failed payments and session timeouts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Create tests/checkout.spec.ts and execute the test suite to ensure system integrity.'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Ship early. Use automated CI/CD pipelines to ensure your code is always in a deployable state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Vercel-compatible deployment configuration. Outline the environment variables required for Stripe, Auth, and Database connectivity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Verify environment variables and trigger a dry-run build process.'

pro_tips: Never ask AI to 'write the app.' Ask it to 'generate the technical requirements document' first to anchor the logic.
Use Claude Code's context awareness by keeping your documentation in a README.md file; it acts as the 'source of truth' for the agent.
Prioritize 'buy' over 'build' for infrastructure. If a service like Clerk or Stripe exists, use their SDKs rather than custom-coding logic.
Treat your code repository like a high-stakes investment portfolio: keep it lean, documented, and regularly audited by AI.
When you hit a bug, feed the error log directly into Claude Code—it performs better with raw data than your human interpretation of the problem.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus immediately on the feedback loop. Use your first 10 users to iterate on the product roadmap before adding complex features.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the technical leverage to build at the speed of thought. Don't let the lack of a CS degree stop you from capturing the market. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Your database is the foundation. If the schema is wrong, the product fails. Define your entities before writing a single line of application logic.
Use AI to build in isolated modules. This ensures if one component breaks, the entire engine doesn't collapse.
